worlds.description: Monastery-fortresses hanging above a world that choked. The chains are ancient, the anchors are cracking, and the abbeys sway in winds that carry poison. The monks pray for structural integrity. It's unclear if anyone listens.

Miles of subterranean archive beneath a collapsed civilization, where the dead are catalogued by clerks who don't recall being hired, shelves extend into geometries suggesting more than three dimensions, and every living thing that enters receives a file.

Reality here renders in flat textures and jagged polygons, a world of low-resolution dread where fog hides draw-distance and nothing casts a proper shadow. Pilgrims seek the impossible libraries built around towering crystal pillars, hoping the glitched grimoires within can explain why existence looks this way—or how to escape it.

worlds.name: Rotting Expanse
worlds.description: Ancient witches claimed this wilderness generations ago, transforming every plant into mold-covered parodies of living things. The air hangs thick with infectious spores that force travelers to wear elaborate filters woven from blessed cloth and copper wire. The witches themselves have merged with the decay, their bodies more fungal network than human flesh, spreading their influence through root and spore.

worlds.name: Blighted Canopy
worlds.description: Deep within an ancient rainforest, shamanic tribes wage a generations-long war against a spreading corruption that twists great apes into hulking, horn-crowned demons. Ivory-skinned apparitions drift between the trees—spirits of the land itself manifesting to guide or deceive those who would cleanse the taint. The shamans paint themselves in ash and bone-dust, channeling the forest's wrath through ritual and sacrifice.

worlds.name: Thawing Pinnacles
worlds.description: For an age beyond counting, the crystalline peaks stood frozen in perfect silence—until the first cracks appeared beneath silver moonlight. Ancient things sealed within the ice have begun to stir, and the mountain tribes must reckon with powers their ancestors imprisoned long ago. Pilgrims now climb toward the fracturing summits, some seeking to restore the stasis, others to welcome what emerges.
